title: Compile-time Debug Mode Flag
prompt_md:
## Goal
Read one integer and print its value. When `debugMode` is `true`, also print a debug label before the value. When `debugMode` is `false`, print only the value.

## Input
- Line 1: one integer N (-1000 Ōēż N Ōēż 1000)

## Output
- When `debugMode` is `true`: print `Debug: N` (where N is the integer), then print `Value: N` on the next line.
- When `debugMode` is `false`: print only `Value: N`.
- (The exercise tests with `debugMode = true`.)

## Requirements
1. Use `if constexpr` with the `constexpr bool debugMode { true }` variable already provided.
2. The debug line must be `Debug: ` followed by the integer with no extra spaces.
3. The value line must be `Value: ` followed by the integer with no extra spaces.
4. Edge cases: N = 0, N = -500.

title: Platform-aware Size Report (complete the function)
prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete the function `void printSizeInfo()` so it uses `if constexpr` to print either `Platform: 64-bit` or `Platform: 32-bit` based on the compile-time constant `platformBits`.

## Input
- No input (the function takes no arguments and reads nothing from stdin).

## Output
- If `platformBits == 64`: print `Platform: 64-bit`
- If `platformBits == 32`: print `Platform: 32-bit`
- (The exercise tests with `platformBits = 64`.)

## Requirements
1. Use `if constexpr` inside `printSizeInfo` on the `constexpr int platformBits` constant.
2. The function must not read from `cin`.
3. The output line must match exactly ŌĆö no extra spaces or punctuation.
